cards.title: Implement the typed memory substrate, deterministic scaffold-only LOG/CONSULT baseline, shadow MemoryActionBroker, and immutable receipts
cards.trace: Linear META-6; charter stages 2,4-5; evidence-before-learning, bounded authority, reversible lineage, evaluator non-self-approval; H/E/W frozen; live context bytes unchanged
next: claim Claude lane (coordinator-reassigned from Codex with user approval)
first checkpoint: typed store and shadow broker with receipts

definition.budget: One bounded implementation card owned by the Claude lane (coordinator-reassigned from the preferred Codex lane with user approval, 2026-07-16). Implement the Phase-2 typed memory substrate: a new src/metaharness/memory package with append-only typed MemoryRecord stores (working/episodic/semantic/procedural kinds), activation lifecycle with evidence-preserving tombstoning, receipted mutation, commit-before-log audit ordering, bounded SpecialistTaskAction authorization, and a memory-skill health circuit breaker; SQLite/WAL-backed durability with schema migrations and FTS5 lexical search; a deterministic scaffold-only LOG/CONSULT baseline operating through a shadow MemoryActionBroker restricted to allowlisted scoped operations (search, read, create_candidate, append, upsert, revise_candidate, link, compress_candidate) that rejects unknown operations, path traversal, cross-scope IDs, immutable-evidence writes, lifecycle bypass, and domain task actions, emitting immutable self-verifying MemoryActionReceipt records; and additive context-contract boundary validators closing META5-MEM-002, 003, 006, 010, 012, 014, and 015. Flip the closed contracts strict xfails to enforced tests and update tests/fixtures/meta5 corpus statuses; META5-MEM-009 and META5-MEM-011 remain strict expected failures reserved for TASK-20260714-008 and TASK-20260714-007. Live prompts, fitted messages, and request bytes remain byte-identical. No memory.training or memory.promotion implementation, no retrieval wiring into workers, no prompt activation, no optimizer, no evaluator evolution, no weight training, no deployment, no playbook migration, and no new runtime authority.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any live prompt, fitted-message, or request-byte change; any grant to a specialist of domain-action commit, visibility-widening, evaluator, promotion, deployment, or self-approval authority; destructive deletion or durable activation exposed as a direct model action; any unreceipted mutation path; weakening or deletion of existing META-4/META-5 test assertions; H/E/W drift; owned-path expansion; nondeterministic scaffold-baseline output; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved independent-review P0/P1 finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Coordinator integration and acceptance require immutable focused and full test evidence plus an independent adversarial review panel (GLM-5.2 frozen-diff, codex CLI, Claude reviewer) with no unresolved P0/P1 findings, and independent MiniMax-M3 behavioral verification with its own scratch probes. An xfail flip counts as implemented capability only with a green enforced test and review acceptance.
definition.nextCheckpoint: The src/metaharness/memory package exists with append-only stores, activation lifecycle, receipted mutation, and commit-ordered audit; the shadow MemoryActionBroker and immutable MemoryActionReceipt validate allowlisted scoped operations; the scaffold-only LOG/CONSULT baseline is deterministic; thirteen META5-MEM contracts are enforced while META5-MEM-009 and META5-MEM-011 remain strict expected failures.

cards.title: Promote the shadow ContextEnvelope to the single live prompt assembler: both worker families consume typed envelope sections, trust rules and redaction enforced on sent bytes, per-attempt manifest journaled as the authoritative what-model-saw record
cards.trace: Linear META-19; charter stages 2-4; full-fidelity evidence, bounded authority, evaluator non-self-approval, reversible lineage; H/E/W frozen; lifts the shadow-era byte-identity freeze as the planned promotion
next: first checkpoint: live assembler consumed by both builders with trust enforcement

definition.budget: One bounded implementation card owned by the Claude lane. Promote the shadow ContextEnvelope to the single live prompt assembler (Linear META-19; the planned promotion of the META-4 shadow staging): add a section-based live assembly entry point in src/metaharness/context that accepts typed declared sections (section_type, source kind, trust, sensitivity, content) and deterministically produces both the chat-message list consumed by OpenAICompatWorker (harness/local.py) and the flat prompt consumed by CodingAgentWorker (harness/coding.py), so both worker families consume envelope sections rather than concatenating ad hoc. Enforce ContextTrust rules on the live path: UNTRUSTED_EVIDENCE and GENERATED_SUMMARY content can never occupy SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS, RESPONSE_CONTRACT, or TOOL_SCHEMAS instruction slots; evaluator receipts never carry INSTRUCTION trust; violations fail closed before any model call. Apply secret/credential redaction to the messages and prompts actually sent, not only the manifest; manifest entries must be derived from the same envelope that produced the live bytes so shadow/live divergence is impossible by construction. Journal the ContextManifest per attempt through the existing run-event sink as a non-shadow context.manifest event so 'what did the model see on attempt N' is answerable from the run journal; assembler failure remains fail-closed for contract violations and must not silently fall back to an unvalidated prompt for trust/redaction failures. The prior shadow-era byte-identity freeze is explicitly lifted by this card: live prompt bytes may change only as required by unified assembly, trust enforcement, and redaction, with tests attesting content parity otherwise. Existing META-4/5/6 test assertions may be updated only where they assert the superseded shadow-only contract, and each such update must cite this card.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any grant of evaluator, merge, promotion, deployment, credential, or self-approval authority; any expansion of specialist or worker runtime authority; H/E/W drift; owned-path expansion; a live message or prompt whose bytes are not derived from the validated envelope (shadow/live divergence); untrusted content accepted into an instruction slot; deletion or weakening of META-4/META-5/META-6 assertions other than superseded shadow-only byte-identity assertions updated with citation; nondeterministic assembly for identical inputs; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved independent-review P0/P1 finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Coordinator integration and acceptance require immutable focused and full test evidence plus an independent adversarial review panel (GLM-5.2 frozen-diff per AGENTS.md, codex CLI read-only, Claude opus reviewer subagent) with no unresolved P0/P1 findings, and independent MiniMax-M3 behavioral verification with its own scratch probes driving trust enforcement, live redaction, both-builder envelope consumption, and per-attempt manifest journaling.
definition.nextCheckpoint: A live section-based assembler exists in src/metaharness/context; harness/local.py and harness/coding.py both consume envelope sections; a test proves untrusted content cannot claim instruction trust on the live path; redaction demonstrably applies to sent bytes; the per-attempt context.manifest journal answers what the model saw on attempt N.

cards.title: Enforce and attest coding-worker execution boundaries, serialize shared workspaces, and gate dispatch with scoped capability tokens
cards.trace: Linear META-18; charter stages 3-4; bounded authority; generated code executes only after explicit isolation and pre-dispatch authorization; H/E/W frozen
next: first checkpoint: failing authorization and workspace-conflict tests

definition.budget: One bounded Codex implementation card for Linear META-18. Establish an explicit, machine-readable execution-boundary contract for every coding CLI adapter without pretending the post-hoc verifier sandbox protects generation: Codex CODE_EDIT uses its enforced native workspace-write mode; subscription Codex remains native read-only; subscription Claude remains plan mode with Read/Glob/Grep; Pi, OpenCode, and edit-capable Claude are explicitly classified as operator-trusted CLI boundaries with no harness filesystem jail. Emit the selected boundary before spawn and document the per-adapter guarantees and limitations. Add a cross-process exclusive lease keyed by the resolved workspace path so concurrent workers cannot mutate or inspect one shared checkout while an attempt is active; separate explicitly supplied git worktrees remain supported, but the harness does not auto-create branches or worktrees. Activate scoped capability tokens at the TaskExecutor pre-dispatch boundary: mint and validate a short-lived token bound to worker, task, task:execute, tier, and task type before attempt assignment/start events and before runner.run; invalid, expired, revoked, wrong-subject, wrong-task, or wrong-scope authorization must fail closed with no worker call, no capability evidence, and reasoned provenance/event evidence. Preserve the existing post-hoc OS-sandboxed verifier, result authenticity checks, routing, budgets, context assembly, human approvals, and adapter behavior outside these explicit boundaries.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any evaluator, merge, promotion, deployment, credential, or runtime-authority grant; H/E/W drift; owned-path expansion; an authorization failure that still invokes a runner or emits attempt.started; a token not bound to the chosen worker and task; a shared-workspace collision that permits concurrent execution; a claim implementation that auto-creates, deletes, resets, or switches git worktrees; documentation that calls operator-trusted Pi/OpenCode/Claude execution sandboxed; credential removal or network denial that breaks authenticated CLI use; weakening the protected verifier sandbox or result-signature checks; failing acceptance commands; or unresolved independent-review P0/P1 findings.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Authorization validity is deterministic signature, expiry, revocation, subject, task, and scope validation. Workspace exclusivity is tested with real overlapping attempts and a cross-process lock probe. Adapter-boundary claims are checked against exact argv and emitted evidence; operator-trusted adapters remain honestly labeled. Coordinator integration requires frozen focused/full test evidence plus a read-only Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 frozen-diff review with no unresolved P0/P1 findings. Human promotion and deployment authority remain unchanged.
definition.nextCheckpoint: Invalid-input tests prove an invalid scoped dispatch token produces zero runner calls and no attempt.started event; overlapping workers targeting one resolved workspace yield one winner and one reasoned workspace_claim denial while distinct worktrees remain independent; every adapter reports an honest execution-boundary classification matching its argv; focused tests pass.

cards.title: Apply the 10 pre-audited selflearn learning-module consistency findings (Linear META-24): EFE staleness-boost inversion, coverage-evidence branches, baseline validity drift, dead high_signal property, untested min_validation_gain gate, and 5 doc/test-drift items — each behavioral fix with a citing test

definition.budget: One bounded direct-fix card owned by the Claude lane applying exactly the 10 pre-audited selflearn learning-module consistency findings from Linear META-24 (episodic todo 20260719-015442). Behavioral: (1) fix expected_free_energy_value so staleness evidence age-in-days is never read as a failure count, restoring the documented quality>staleness ranking in Learner.suggestions(), with a staleness-EFE test; (2) fix gap_signals coverage-evidence branches (unreachable not-in-coverage branch; covered-but-nothing-retrieved mislabel); (3) _valid_baseline imports BASELINE_FILE from regression.py and reports an accurate reason when the baseline exists but injected is not True; (4) delete or unify the dead EvaluationCriterion.high_signal property; (5) add a test for the min_validation_gain eligibility gate. Doc/test drift: (6) advisor.py module docstring matches emitted priorities; (7) learning/__init__.py docstring matches the exported API; (8) marks.py header credits injected + seeded_by plan seeds; (9) remove private _parse_iso from marks.py __all__; (10) make test_suggestions_efe_ranked_and_readonly honest about suggestions() not being read-only (rename or assert real behavior). Each behavioral fix lands with a citing test. No other selflearn behavior changes; items listed as verified-clean in META-24 are not re-audited.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside the owned paths (in particular any src/metaharness/ path — META-18/TASK-20260719-014 owns the execution-environment scope, including docs/architecture.md); any grant of evaluator, merge, promotion, deployment, credential, or self-approval authority; H/E/W drift; any behavior change beyond the 10 enumerated findings; owned-path expansion; re-auditing or modifying the verified-clean areas (split-sealing, per-item regression veto, frozen-evaluator identity, GAP_KINDS/ACTIONS mapping, half-life plumbing, doctor parse_state_data).
definition.evaluatorAuthority: Direct-fix mode user-approved 2026-07-19 (small, pre-audited scope): the full multi-seat quality process is waived. The implementing seat still cannot self-accept: coordinator acceptance requires immutable focused and full test receipts at the reviewed head, a clean git diff --check, and the user-reviewed merged PR as the independent gate before Done.
definition.nextCheckpoint: All 10 findings applied with citing tests; focused learning/advisor suites green; full selflearn suite green; full repo suite green (1230 passed / 1 skipped / 2 xfailed baseline, plus new tests); branch pushed and PR open.

cards.title: Attest per-tool MCP schema provenance without granting instruction trust
cards.trace: Linear META-23; charter stage 2; bounded authority and full-fidelity per-tool provenance; H change with E/W frozen
next: first checkpoint: failing mixed-origin manifest tests

definition.budget: One bounded Codex-lane implementation card for Linear META-23. Preserve ToolSpec's exact local or mcp:<server> origin through deterministic OpenAI schema rendering into the live context manifest. Add a provenance-aware schema record/draft path without changing the existing openai_schemas() return contract or the provider-facing tools payload. Emit one manifest entry per transmitted tool schema so mixed local and MCP schemas retain separate source kind, exact stable source identity, trust, redaction, hashes, and receipts; reconstruction must remain byte-identical to the transmitted schema list. Local caller-authored policy schemas remain TOOL_POLICY_SCHEMA/INSTRUCTION. MCP schemas are a dedicated external/MCP source kind with UNTRUSTED_EVIDENCE, and typed model validation forbids laundering that source kind to INSTRUCTION. Preserve deterministic ordering/deduplication, schema redaction, aggregate wire-byte budgeting, tool execution, tool-round behavior, manifest hashing, historical manifest validation, and all routing/evaluator/deployment authority outside this H-plane provenance correction. E and W remain frozen.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any provider tools-payload change, MCP load/call regression, schema order or deduplication drift, manifest reconstruction divergence, loss of exact MCP server/tool identity, MCP schema attested as INSTRUCTION, source-controlled trust, provenance metadata entering provider bytes or budget counts, secret leakage, historical manifest-hash incompatibility, unrelated context/memory behavior change, evaluator/merge/promotion/deployment/credential/runtime authority grant, owned-path expansion, failing acceptance command, or unresolved independent-review P0/P1 finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Deterministic tests must prove exact wire compatibility, mixed-origin per-tool reconstruction, MCP trust non-escalation, redaction, budget parity, round-to-round provenance, and invalid-input rejection. The Codex orchestrator inspects the exact diff and test evidence. A distinct coordinator may integrate only after a read-only Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 review of the immutable base/head diff reports no unresolved P0/P1 findings. Human promotion and deployment authority remain unchanged.

claimSnapshot.budget: One bounded Codex-lane implementation card for Linear META-23. Preserve ToolSpec's exact local or mcp:<server> origin through deterministic OpenAI schema rendering into the live context manifest. Preserve deterministic ordering/deduplication, schema redaction, aggregate wire-byte budgeting, tool execution, tool-round behavior, manifest hashing, historical manifest validation, and all routing/evaluator/deployment authority outside this H-plane provenance correction. E and W remain frozen.

cards.title: Implement the isolated discovery supervisor, lineage recovery, typed knowledge hub, and role-specific context manifests
cards.trace: Linear META-7; charter stages 2-4; H-only discovery MVP; E/W and protected evaluator frozen; bounded authority and reversible lineage

definition.budget: One bounded H-only MVP in a new metaharness.discovery package. Implement frozen self-hashed campaign/lifecycle/terminal contracts; an exact-parent LineageWorkspaceManager that creates isolated Git worktrees, checkpoints immutable child commits, and recovers or quarantines ambiguous state without destructive cleanup; an append-only typed DiscoveryKnowledgeHub with private/lineage/island/campaign/reviewed-project visibility, untrusted candidate defaults, progressive disclosure, and use receipts; role-specific context manifests for fresh explorers, direct-lineage optimizers, and compact schedulers; and an asynchronous CampaignSupervisor with injected worker and opaque proxy-evaluator ports, bounded FIFO concurrency/restarts/timeouts, durable intent/outcome journaling, idempotent resume, and exactly one terminal receipt per attempt. Reuse frozen context/scope/hash contracts and existing honest workspace-boundary primitives. H changes only inside the owned discovery package and tests; E and W remain frozen. No live prompt wiring or existing product-file changes.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any need to edit outside owned paths; access to protected evaluator cases or internals; proxy/protected evaluator identity conflation; evaluator, promotion, merge, deployment, memory-activation, weight-training, permission-expansion, heartbeat, population-policy, SearchPolicyDSL, island-migration, or multi-parent behavior; any destructive deletion or overwrite of an unowned, dirty, symlinked, ambiguous, or active workspace; shell-interpolated Git refs or path traversal; journal corruption or recovery that cannot be replayed idempotently; cross-project/campaign/lineage knowledge leakage; agent-authored content gaining instruction, verified-fact, active-memory, or reviewed-project authority; E/W drift; unreceipted terminal path; nondeterministic hashes/views for identical inputs; failing acceptance commands; or unresolved independent-review P0/P1 findings.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: Candidate workers receive only an opaque proxy-evaluator port over immutable checkpoint references. Protected evaluator definitions, cases, promotion state, deployment pointers, active memory, and E/W mutation remain outside worker and discovery-kernel authority. Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 reviews the frozen diff read-only; a distinct coordinator verifies and accepts the integrated commit. No component may self-approve.

cards.title: Add typed heartbeats, population scheduling, and declarative search-policy candidates
cards.trace: Linear META-8; charter stages 2-4; heartbeats carry observations/memory-skill candidates/redirect proposals but never authority changes; bounded SearchPolicyDSL with schema/static/simulation/shadow validation; no hot-swapped LLM-generated controller code; E/W frozen

definition.budget: H-only extension of the META-7 discovery kernel, confined to src/metaharness/discovery/ and its four owned test modules. Implement frozen self-hashed population descriptors/fingerprints; a deterministic PopulationScheduler emitting SearchDecisionReceipts (parent/role/variation/briefing/budget/alternatives/expected gain) under diversity floors and baseline reseeding; typed HeartbeatEngine checkpoints (reflection/consolidation/redirection on event/evaluation/time/plateau triggers with epsilon + cooldown) whose outputs are only untrusted CANDIDATE artifacts or queued proposals; a declarative SearchPolicyDSL + SearchPolicySnapshot with strict SCHEMA->STATIC->SIMULATION->SHADOW validation; and a SearchPolicyEvolver with frozen windows, strategy history, four-receipt-gated activation, and parent fallback that preserves the candidate population. No hot-swapped LLM-generated controller code. E and W remain frozen; no live prompt wiring and no changes to existing product files outside the owned discovery package.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on any need to edit outside the owned discovery package and its four test modules; any heartbeat, scheduler, policy, or evolver path minting or carrying promotion, deploy, evaluator-write, memory-activation, weight-training, or permission-expansion authority; any component suppressing protected capture or activating its own output; a policy proposer scoring, approving, or activating itself; any validation-stage failure that does not restore the parent policy with the candidate population untouched; any SearchPolicyDSL construct able to carry executable code, imports, tool calls, evaluator logic, permissions, or pointer mutations; any receipt binding fewer fields than it claims; any decision path where identical inputs yield different bytes, or that reads a wall clock or randomness; E/W drift; dishonest termination or limitation docstrings; failing acceptance commands; or unresolved independent-review P0/P1 findings.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: No heartbeat, scheduler, policy, or evolver path may mint or carry promotion, deployment, evaluator-write, memory-activation, weight-training, or permission-expansion authority. Heartbeat outputs are untrusted CANDIDATE artifacts or queued proposals only. A policy proposer may not score, approve, or activate itself; activation requires four independent validation receipts (SCHEMA/STATIC/SIMULATION/SHADOW). Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 reviews the frozen diff read-only; a distinct coordinator verifies and accepts the integrated commit. No component may self-approve.
acceptanceCommands: /Users/charltondho/Developer/projects/meta-harness/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_discovery_models.py tests/test_discovery_lineage.py tests/test_discovery_knowledge.py tests/test_discovery_contexts.py tests/test_discovery_supervisor.py tests/adversarial/test_discovery_boundaries.py
definition.nextCheckpoint: The owned discovery package and its disjoint tests prove deterministic population scheduling under diversity floors and enforced stop rules, typed heartbeat checkpoints whose outputs carry no authority and whose resource accounting is cumulative, a SearchPolicyDSL structurally unable to carry code or authority, strict SCHEMA->STATIC->SIMULATION->SHADOW validation that fails closed to the parent policy, and receipts whose self-hashes bind every field they claim -- with identical inputs producing identical bytes on every decision path.

cards.title: compile learned selflearn workflow entries to cross-validated executors
cards.trace: Linear META-17; charter stages 4-5 (governed learning to reusable procedural knowledge); workflow entry remains the verified spec, executor is a derived artifact; evaluator non-self-approval, bounded authority, reversible lineage, honest termination; H/E/W frozen. CARD RECONSTRUCTED 2026-07-23 after control-root data loss (see evidence episode); definition hash verified byte-identical to the frozen dispatch value.

definition.budget: One bounded claude-lane card for Linear META-17 adding a new selflearn compilation package with six modules: models.py (frozen self-hashed ExecutorSpec/ExecutorCandidate/IndependentTestSuite/CrossValidationReceipt contracts content-hash-bound to the source workflow entry procedure), compiler.py (WorkflowCompiler generating an executable deterministic spine from a published workflow entry: steps to functions, depends_on to control flow, step checks to assertions, approval-type steps to hard stops awaiting human input), testgen.py (WorkflowTestAuthor generating validation tests from the spec alone, never seeing executor bytes, identity enforced distinct from the compiler reusing the evalgen author!=validator mechanism), gate.py (CrossValidationGate running executor against the independent tests in the ExecutionPort sandbox; quarantine-by-default; sandbox pass plus strict-mode approval activates; failure rejects the executor and leaves the spec untouched; every gate decision journaled as a frozen receipt), runtime.py (ExecutorRuntime executing activated executors and emitting TaskOutcome records whose step_ids reference actually-executed steps, feeding the existing learning/marks machinery so failing steps harm the entry precisely), registry.py (hash-bound registry mapping entry content-hash to activated executor with staleness query). Existing-file edits limited to doctor.py (flag executors whose bound hash no longer matches their spec) and __init__.py exports. Tests land only in the three reserved new test files. The workflow entry remains the source of truth; the compiler never scores, approves, or activates its own output; H/E/W frozen; no live metaharness prompt/evaluator/deployment wiring; no changes to contracts.py, store, learning, verification, or retrieval modules.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside the reserved paths; compiler and test-author sharing identity or the test-author observing executor bytes; an executor becoming runnable without a sandbox pass receipt plus recorded strict-mode approval; any construct letting generated executor code mutate the spec, store, marks, regression baselines, permissions, promotion state, or deployment pointers; generated code carrying network, credential, subprocess, or authority-minting capability beyond the ExecutionPort sandbox; a gate decision lacking a frozen journaled receipt; E/W drift; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved P0/P1 review finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: Builder seats (pi/MiniMax-M3 via herdr, bare --no-extensions) write product source and tests but never commit, push, or self-approve. Provider-diverse frozen-diff review (pi/GLM-5.2 via neuralwatt, read-only) precedes submission; the mandatory AGENTS.md gate (Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 on the frozen base..head diff, findings P0-P3 with file:line evidence) precedes Review. Inside the product, the cross-validation gate is the only activation path: an independently authored test suite (identity distinct from compiler) must pass in the sandbox and strict-mode approval must be recorded; the compiler can never approve, score, or activate its own output. The orchestrator triages every finding, personally inspects the final diff, and a distinct coordinator actor runs integrate and acceptance. Protected evaluator definitions, promotion state, deployment pointers, and E/W mutation remain outside all seats' authority.
definition.nextCheckpoint: Failing tests first prove: a workflow entry compiles to an executor content-hash-bound to its procedure (spec edit forces regeneration); the test-author never sees executor bytes and compiler/test-author identity collision is rejected; an executor is unrunnable until sandbox tests pass and strict-mode approval is recorded; approval-type steps become hard stops awaiting human input; a runtime run emits TaskOutcome records whose step_ids reference executed steps and feed learning/marks; doctor flags an executor whose spec hash drifted; a tamper sweep over executor and gate receipts rejects every mutation; the full selflearn suite stays at or above the 224-passed+1-env-skip baseline.

cards.title: Repair validateIntegrationReceipt so a card integrated before another card's board transition can still be accepted, while still rejecting an integrationReceipt ahead of the board
cards.trace: Discovered during META-17/META-8 acceptance 2026-07-24: global-revision equality strands any card whose accept does not immediately follow its own integrate; TASK-20260723-017 is currently unacceptable by any legal transition

definition.budget: Single-predicate repair to the atomic Kanban control root. validateIntegrationReceipt currently requires receipt.revisionTo === expectedRevision. Because expectedRevision is the GLOBAL board revision, any transition on any OTHER card between a card's integrate and its accept permanently strands that card in verifying: revisions are monotonic, so the required revision can never recur, and block (the only other exit from verifying) applies the identical check. This is not hypothetical -- TASK-20260723-017 (META-17) integrated 102->103, TASK-20260714-006 (META-8) integrated 103->104, and META-17 is now unacceptable by any legal transition. The equality test provides no card-level staleness protection that the status machine does not already guarantee: while card.status === 'verifying' the latest receipt is necessarily the integration receipt, since the only exits from verifying are accept (->done) and block (->blocked) and both are gated on that status. The genuine threat the check must retain is an integrationReceipt claiming a revision AHEAD of the board, which indicates forgery or corruption. Change the predicate from strict inequality to receipt.revisionTo > expectedRevision. Scope is scripts/workplan.mjs plus regression tests in scripts/workplan.test.mjs. No product source, no selflearn, no discovery package, no prompt/evaluator/deployment wiring.
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside scripts/workplan.mjs and scripts/workplan.test.mjs; any change that weakens single-winner claim arbitration, fencing, path reservation, lock classification, or actor/owner separation; any relaxation permitting an integrationReceipt whose revisionTo exceeds the board revision; any change to the append-only receipt chain, receipt hashing, or revision monotonicity; any hand-edit of .workplan/state.json; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved P0/P1 review finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat may not self-approve. Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 reviews the frozen base..head diff read-only and returns P0-P3 findings with file:line evidence, preserved verbatim under .review-store/ per AGENTS.md. A distinct coordinator actor runs integrate and accept, and per the defect this card fixes, accept must follow integrate with no intervening board transition until the fix itself is accepted. This card grants no promotion, deployment, evaluator-write, or credential authority.
definition.nextCheckpoint: A card integrated before another card's board transition can still be accepted; an integrationReceipt whose revisionTo exceeds the board revision is still rejected; block-from-verifying and resume-from-blocked-verifying both tolerate an intervening transition on an unrelated card; and every pre-existing workplan test still passes.

cards.title: Bind card.integrationReceipt to the append-only ledger so a swapped or replayed receipt is detectable independent of the revision bound, and close the resume/retainPaths coverage gap
cards.trace: Three deferred P3s from the TASK-20260724-018 panel. GLM P3-1: card.integrationReceipt is a standalone snapshot; appendReceipt creates un-chained records (workplan.mjs:1292-1313) and validateState does not bind a verifying card to a fresh receipt, so a hand-swapped receipt is caught only by reviewFreeze correlation. Codex P3: the post-drift block test neither resumes the card nor exercises retainPaths=false, leaving the recovery path unpinned. GLM P3-C: scope the new comment invariant against direct state.json tampering.

cards.title: Guard the unjournalled ValueError escape from ast.parse on NUL-bearing source in the selflearn compilation runtime
cards.trace: META-17 gate-6 finding F1 (P2, pre-existing). _ast_preflight calls ast.parse at an unguarded call site (runtime.py:275); ast.parse raises ValueError -- not SyntaxError -- on NUL-bearing source, escaping every guard unjournalled. Only reachable via a tampered hash-matched file since json.dumps escapes NUL in legitimate compiler output. The path-resolution subclass of this escape class was closed in 5d2fb52; this source-NUL subclass was not, so the class is NOT exhaustively closed.

definition.budget: Close the unjournalled-ValueError escape class at the selflearn compilation runtime's AST preflight (META-17 gate-6 finding F1, P2, pre-existing). _ast_preflight (selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation/runtime.py:36) calls ast.parse at line 45 catching only SyntaxError; ast.parse raises ValueError -- not SyntaxError -- on NUL-bearing source, so at the call site (runtime.py:277) that ValueError escapes run() raw and unjournalled, violating both the journalled-refusal evidence contract and the RuntimeCompError normalization contract. The path-resolution subclass of this escape class was closed in 5d2fb52 (control-character preflight plus (OSError, ValueError) on the read guards, journalled under executor.malformed-path); this source-NUL subclass at the AST preflight was explicitly left open. Only reachable via a tampered hash-matched executor file, since json.dumps escapes NUL in legitimate compiler output -- which is precisely the adversarial surface the tamper evidence record exists for. Required behaviour: a preflight failure on NUL-bearing or otherwise ValueError-raising source must journal an executor refusal via the established _journal_refusal mechanism under an honest kind consistent with the existing executor.* refusal kinds, then raise RuntimeCompError; no raw ValueError may escape run() from the preflight site. Bringing the preflight's existing SyntaxError and dunder/builtins refusal paths under the same journalled-refusal contract at that call site is in scope if the chosen guard shape covers them naturally; rewriting the preflight's detection logic is not. Add regression tests proving: NUL-bearing source with a matching hash journals a refusal and raises RuntimeCompError (not ValueError); legitimate executor sources still compile and run unchanged; and the refusal journal entry carries the entry_id and honest kind. Scope is exactly the reserved paths: selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation (runtime.py expected; sibling modules only if the guard genuinely requires a shared helper) and selflearn/tests/test_compilation_runtime.py. No workplan scripts, no discovery package, no prompt/evaluator/deployment wiring, no doctor.py (its NUL-path reporting gap is a separate recorded P3, out of scope).
definition.stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside the two reserved paths; any weakening of the existing preflight refusals (dunder access, __builtins__ reference), the executor hash-tamper check, the restricted-globals sandbox, or any existing executor.* refusal journalling; any change that lets a preflight or sandbox failure skip journalling; any new dependency; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved P0/P1 review finding.
definition.evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat may not self-approve. Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2 reviews the frozen base..head diff read-only per AGENTS.md and returns P0-P3 findings with file:line evidence, preserved verbatim under .review-store/. Any P0/P1 requires a new commit and a fresh frozen-diff review; after any fix round, re-freeze the card to the head actually reviewed before submit. A distinct coordinator actor runs integrate and accept. This card grants no promotion, deployment, evaluator-write, or credential authority.
definition.nextCheckpoint: A hash-matched executor file containing NUL bytes is refused with a journalled executor refusal and RuntimeCompError instead of an unjournalled raw ValueError; every legitimate executor source compiles and runs exactly as before; all pre-existing selflearn and root suites stay green; and the ValueError escape class at the AST preflight call site is closed with regression tests pinning it shut.

claimSnapshot.budget: Close the unjournalled-ValueError escape class at the selflearn compilation runtime's AST preflight (META-17 gate-6 finding F1, P2, pre-existing). _ast_preflight (selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation/runtime.py:36) calls ast.parse at line 45 catching only SyntaxError; ast.parse raises ValueError -- not SyntaxError -- on NUL-bearing source, so at the call site (runtime.py:277) that ValueError escapes run() raw and unjournalled, violating both the journalled-refusal evidence contract and the RuntimeCompError normalization contract. The path-resolution subclass of this escape class was closed in 5d2fb52 (control-character preflight plus (OSError, ValueError) on the read guards, journalled under executor.malformed-path); this source-NUL subclass at the AST preflight was explicitly left open. Only reachable via a tampered hash-matched executor file, since json.dumps escapes NUL in legitimate compiler output -- which is precisely the adversarial surface the tamper evidence record exists for. Bringing the preflight's existing SyntaxError and dunder/builtins refusal paths under the same journalled-refusal contract at that call site is in scope if the chosen guard shape covers them naturally; rewriting the preflight's detection logic is not. Add regression tests proving: NUL-bearing source with a matching hash journals a refusal and raises RuntimeCompError (not ValueError); legitimate executor sources still compile and run unchanged; and the refusal journal entry carries the entry_id and honest kind. Scope is exactly the reserved paths: selflearn/src/selflearn/compilation (runtime.py expected; sibling modules only if the guard genuinely requires a shared helper) and selflearn/tests/test_compilation_runtime.py. No workplan scripts, no discovery package, no prompt/evaluator/deployment wiring, no doctor.py (its NUL-path reporting gap is a separate recorded P3, out of scope).

cards.title: Add a generic pi owner namespace so pi-driven open-weights seats can claim and own workplan cards under their real identity
cards.trace: User decision 2026-07-24: generic pi namespace (not per-provider). The control root hard-codes claimable owner namespaces to codex|claude: the claim owner regex (workplan.mjs:192), the allowedOwnerNamespaces whitelist (workplan.mjs:1177,1183), and the remote gateway set {codex,claude,dev-orchestrator} (development/remote_workplan/gateway.py:235). A pi seat can execute the claim CLI but can only present a false codex/claude identity, which the append-only ledger honesty rules forbid. Add pi to all three enforcement points plus regression tests, and add a pi lane label to AGENTS.md:38. Provider identity stays visible via the session segment of pi:<host>:<session> naming convention. Cards already Ready keep their frozen namespaces (t020 remains claude-only).
